summary.intent: Implement a native `read_skill` tool for the moltis agent platform so skills can be activated reliably without depending on the model inferring to use `exec`+`cat`, then iterate on PR review feedback until Greptile reaches 5/5 confidence, and finally add comprehensive regression tests to prevent future breakage.
summary.outcome: PR #630 merged-ready with 5 commits: `ReadSkillTool` with full safety guards, `safety.rs` prompt-injection scanner, updated `prompt_gen.rs`, gateway registration, hermes-agent feature parity (binary detection, `assets/` subdir, per-subdir caps, frontmatter surfacing), 4 Greptile iterations fixing 9 comments, and a final regression test pass covering discoverer↔prompt parity, create→read and write→read round-trips, hot-discovery, plugin-as-file sidecar rejection, and shared `SIDECAR_SUBDIRS` constant to prevent drift.

repo: Skills were never reliably end-to-end functional before this PR — the original design (Jan 31 2026, commit d4744381) injected an absolute `path=` attribute into `<available_skills>` and instructed the model to 'read its SKILL.md file' with no named tool, relying on smart models to infer `exec`+`cat`.
The only place `SkillContent::load_skill` is called in the runtime is `crates/cli/src/main.rs` for `moltis skills info <name>` — it was never wired into the chat/gateway/agent loop.
`CreateSkillTool`, `UpdateSkillTool`, `DeleteSkillTool`, and `WriteSkillFilesTool` all existed before `ReadSkillTool`; the read side was simply never implemented.
Gateway's `FsSkillDiscoverer` uses `default_paths()` for all other skill tools but the new `ReadSkillTool` needed `default_paths_for(data_dir)` to stay consistent — mismatch was a Greptile finding.

The `SIDECAR_SUBDIRS` list (`references/`, `templates/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`) existed in two places (skill_tools.rs and prompt_gen.rs) and was consolidated into a single constant in `crates/skills/src/lib.rs` to make drift a compile-time error.
`just lint` fails on macOS due to a pre-existing llama-cpp-sys CUDA build error (tracked as moltis-csq) — targeted `cargo clippy -p <crate>` is the reliable alternative.
Beads (the project's issue tracker) is used for task tracking with issue IDs like `moltis-6p0`, `moltis-5xp`, etc.; issues are claimed before work starts and closed when done.
The `local-llm-metal` feature flag is needed for gateway clippy on macOS.

code.finding: ReadSkillTool dispatches two paths: plugin-backed (single `.md` file) and directory-backed (`SKILL.md` + sidecar subdirs). Plugin-backed skills must reject `file_path` sidecar requests with a clear error since there is no sidecar directory.
Prompt-injection scanner ported from hermes-agent with 9 patterns; runs warn-only (does not block), consistent with hermes behavior.

The `path=` attribute and absolute SKILL.md path leak were deliberately removed from `<available_skills>`; the activation instruction now names `read_skill(name=..., file_path=...)` explicitly. Sidecar subdir list is now generated from `moltis_skills::SIDECAR_SUBDIRS`.

ReadSkillTool registered in the skill-tools block using `FsSkillDiscoverer::default_paths_for(data_dir)` to match the scope used by write tools — not `default_paths()` which uses a different resolution path.

Integration tests grew from 5 to 14 cases across the PR lifecycle, covering: tool presence in registry, primary read, sidecar read, unknown-name error, traversal rejection, assets/ listing, binary handling, discoverer↔prompt parity, tool-name parity, hot-discovery, create→read round-trip, write_files→read round-trip, plugin-as-file sidecar rejection.
New `FsSkillDiscoverer::default_paths_for(data_dir)` method added with direct unit tests; `default_paths()` is the older variant that may resolve differently.

workflow: Greploop pattern: iterate on Greptile review comments until 5/5 confidence + 0 unresolved threads, triggering Greptile re-review after each commit batch.
Background task notifications are unreliable summary labels — a task reported 'exit code 0' but was actually the pre-existing lint failure; always verify with direct status queries.
Clippy `expect_used` lint fires on `.expect()` calls in async contexts — replace with explicit error mapping or `serde_json::Map` construction to avoid it.
When adding a subdir to a sidecar system, export a shared constant rather than duplicating the list across crates — drift is otherwise invisible until a model silently omits a dir from its prompt.
Greptile tends to find new issues each iteration as earlier ones are resolved; 4 iterations were needed to go from 4/5 to 5/5.
Per-subdir caps (e.g. `MAX_SIDECAR_FILES_PER_SUBDIR = 8`) prevent a heavy subdir from silently hiding others under a shared global cap.

friction: `just lint` always fails on macOS due to pre-existing llama-cpp-sys CUDA build error (moltis-csq), requiring manual targeted clippy invocations per crate.
Greptile required 4 iteration cycles with manual re-trigger and polling between each — background polling tasks accumulated stale notifications.
Background task notification summaries were misleading (one reported 'exit code 0' for a run that actually failed with exit code 101).
Dolt remote not configured in the worktree — `bd dolt pull` failed; commit is local-only and not synced to any Dolt remote.

The shared `SIDECAR_SUBDIRS` constant required touching both `crates/skills/src/lib.rs` and `crates/tools/src/skill_tools.rs` to eliminate the drift, since the skills crate doesn't depend on tools and tools depends on skills — the constant had to live in skills and be imported into tools.
open_items: moltis-avv: token-budget fallback for skills prompt — left open per plan spec, not addressed in this PR.
`skills_list` and `skills_categories` progressive-disclosure tools (hermes-agent pattern) — documented as out-of-scope; maps to moltis-avv.
Platform gating for skills (needs frontmatter spec change) — not implemented.
Required env var validation for skills — not implemented.
Slashy path resolution for skill activation — not implemented.
Full `just lint` passing on macOS — blocked by pre-existing moltis-csq (llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure), unrelated to this work.
